title: Function Signatures Schema
description: Defines the structure for function signature lists passed between orchestrator phases. Enforced by scripts/validate_function_signatures.py.

module_id.description: Module identifier, e.g. M01, M02. Must match the module-id pattern used throughout the project.

module_name.description: Human-readable module name in the project's language.

functions.description: All public functions/methods exposed by this module. Private helpers must NOT be listed.

name.description: Function identifier. Must be a valid identifier in the target language.

signature.description: Full function signature as it appears in source code. Used by the test generator to determine call syntax.

parameters.description: Ordered list of parameters. Order must match the signature string.

type.description: Type annotation as declared in the source. E.g. 'str', 'int', 'List[User]', 'Optional[dict]'.

required.description: If false, the parameter has a default value and may be omitted.

default.description: The default value expression. Only present when required=false.

constraints.description: Human-readable constraint descriptions extracted from the spec. E.g. 'non-empty', 'positive integer', 'valid email'.

allowed_values.description: Explicit list of allowed values (for enums / literals).

return_type.description: Return type annotation. Use 'None' / 'void' for procedures.

trigger.description: Condition that causes this exception. Must reference a contract clause.

contract_reference.description: Clause reference in the spec, e.g. §3.2

preconditions.description: Conditions that must be true before calling this function.

side_effects.description: Observable side effects (file writes, network calls, state mutations).
